project.name: IrishTrees
project.summary: Explore Ireland's changing native habitats from post-glacial forests to modern rewilding gardens.

timeline.title: Post-Glacial Pioneer Landscapes
timeline.shortSummary: Bare ground, lakes, wetlands and open scrub began to fill with pioneer plants as the ice retreated.
timeline.gardenLesson: Create colonisation zones: bare soil, damp corners, logs, native shrubs and pond edges.

timeline.title: Pine, Birch, Hazel And Early Wildwood
timeline.shortSummary: Warmer early Holocene conditions supported expanding woodland, including pine, birch and hazel.
timeline.gardenLesson: A small pine-birch-heath grove can echo ancient habitat structure without needing many trees.

timeline.title: Atlantic Mixed Woodland
timeline.shortSummary: Ireland's woodlands became richer regional mosaics of oak, elm, hazel, alder, ash, birch, holly and rowan.
timeline.gardenLesson: Build layers: canopy, shrub, ground flora, deadwood, water and leaf litter.

timeline.title: Farming Arrives And Woodland Opens
timeline.shortSummary: Neolithic farming introduced clearance, grazing, crops and more permanent human influence.
timeline.gardenLesson: Value sunny openings, rough grass, meadow strips, hedges and scrub as much as trees.

timeline.title: Bog, Heath And Upland Mosaics Expand
timeline.shortSummary: Wetter conditions and land-use change helped peatlands, heathlands and upland mosaics expand.
timeline.gardenLesson: Add wet habitat: a fish-free pond, rain garden or damp native planting area.

timeline.title: Medieval Woods, Coppice And Wood Pasture
timeline.shortSummary: Woodlands were increasingly managed for timber, charcoal, grazing, building and estate use.
timeline.gardenLesson: Gentle management such as coppicing, hedge-laying and deadwood can create biodiversity.

timeline.title: Modern Decline And Plantation Landscapes
timeline.shortSummary: Native woodland, bogs and meadows declined, while forestry cover later increased mostly through plantations.
timeline.gardenLesson: Gardens can become habitat corridors when many small spaces connect.

timeline.title: Rewilding And Restoration
timeline.shortSummary: Restoration projects are rebuilding peatlands, native woods, wetlands, meadows and wildlife corridors.
timeline.gardenLesson: Mow less, stop pesticides, add water, plant native layers and leave some places undisturbed.

caseStudies.title: Wild Nephin National Park
caseStudies.summary: A large western Irish national park protecting Atlantic blanket bog, heath, rivers, lakes and upland habitats.
caseStudies.lesson: Large-scale restoration protects whole processes: water, peat, open habitat, rivers and wildlife.

caseStudies.title: Dunsany Nature Reserve
caseStudies.summary: A private Irish estate-scale rewilding project allowing nature to recover across former managed land.
caseStudies.lesson: Reduced intervention and chemical-free management can bring visible biodiversity gains.

caseStudies.title: Knepp Wildland
caseStudies.summary: A major rewilding example where former farmland became a dynamic mosaic of scrub, grassland, wetland and woodland.
caseStudies.lesson: Natural processes can create complex habitat faster than conventional planting plans.

caseStudies.title: All-Ireland Pollinator Plan
caseStudies.summary: A practical evidence-led programme showing how gardens, schools, businesses and communities can help pollinators.
caseStudies.lesson: Simple changes such as mowing less, avoiding pesticides and protecting nesting habitat scale across a landscape.
